sections.heading: Surveillance
sections.content:
Visits every 2–4 months for 2 years after primary treatment, then every 3–6 months for the next 3 years, and annually thereafter [OV-6].
Physical exam including pelvic exam as clinically indicated at each visit [OV-6].
Tumor markers (e.g., CA-125) if initially elevated, measured at each visit [OV-6].

Chest/abdomen/pelvis (C/A/P) CT scan as clinically indicated for symptoms or rising CA-125 [OV-A, Table 2].
PET/CT or MRI for indeterminate findings or high-risk recurrence [OV-A].
For low-grade serous carcinoma, surveillance may include more frequent imaging due to indolent course [LCOC-8].
For malignant germ cell tumors, surveillance includes C/A/P CT and tumor markers per schedule [LCOC-B].

CBC and chemistry profile as indicated during chemotherapy and follow-up [OV-4, OV-6].
CA-125 or other tumor markers (e.g., inhibin for granulosa cell tumors) if elevated at diagnosis [OV-1, LCOC-12].
Genetic testing results (BRCA, HRD) to inform maintenance therapy decisions [OV-C].

Long-term wellness care including cancer screening for secondary malignancies [OV-6, NCCN Guidelines for Survivorship].
Symptom management for chronic toxicities (e.g., neuropathy, fatigue) [OV-D].
Psychosocial support and distress screening [OV-4].

timeline.title: First 2 years after primary treatment
timeline.body: Visits every 2–4 months [OV-6]. Physical exam including pelvic exam as clinically indicated at each visit [OV-6]. Tumor markers (e.g., CA-125) if initially elevated, measured at each visit [OV-6]. Chest/abdomen/pelvis CT scan as clinically indicated for symptoms or rising CA-125 [OV-A, Table 2]. PET/CT or MRI for indeterminate findings or high-risk recurrence [OV-A]. For low-grade serous carcinoma, surveillance may include more frequent imaging due to indolent course [LCOC-8]. For malignant germ cell tumors, surveillance includes C/A/P CT and tumor markers per schedule [LCOC-B]. CBC and chemistry profile as indicated during follow-up [OV-4, OV-6]. CA-125 or other tumor markers if elevated at diagnosis [OV-1, LCOC-12]. Genetic testing results to inform maintenance therapy decisions [OV-C]. Long-term wellness care including cancer screening for secondary malignancies [OV-6, NCCN Guidelines for Survivorship]. Symptom management for chronic toxicities (e.g., neuropathy, fatigue) [OV-D]. Psychosocial support and distress screening [OV-4].

timeline.title: Years 3–5 after primary treatment
timeline.body: Visits every 3–6 months for the next 3 years [OV-6]. Physical exam including pelvic exam as clinically indicated at each visit [OV-6]. Tumor markers (e.g., CA-125) if initially elevated, measured at each visit [OV-6]. Chest/abdomen/pelvis CT scan as clinically indicated for symptoms or rising CA-125 [OV-A, Table 2]. PET/CT or MRI for indeterminate findings or high-risk recurrence [OV-A]. For low-grade serous carcinoma, surveillance may include more frequent imaging due to indolent course [LCOC-8]. For malignant germ cell tumors, surveillance includes C/A/P CT and tumor markers per schedule [LCOC-B]. CBC and chemistry profile as indicated during follow-up [OV-4, OV-6]. CA-125 or other tumor markers if elevated at diagnosis [OV-1, LCOC-12]. Genetic testing results to inform maintenance therapy decisions [OV-C]. Long-term wellness care including cancer screening for secondary malignancies [OV-6, NCCN Guidelines for Survivorship]. Symptom management for chronic toxicities (e.g., neuropathy, fatigue) [OV-D]. Psychosocial support and distress screening [OV-4].

timeline.title: Annually after 5 years
timeline.body: Annual visits thereafter [OV-6]. Physical exam including pelvic exam as clinically indicated at each visit [OV-6]. Tumor markers (e.g., CA-125) if initially elevated, measured at each visit [OV-6]. Chest/abdomen/pelvis CT scan as clinically indicated for symptoms or rising CA-125 [OV-A, Table 2]. PET/CT or MRI for indeterminate findings or high-risk recurrence [OV-A]. For low-grade serous carcinoma, surveillance may include more frequent imaging due to indolent course [LCOC-8]. For malignant germ cell tumors, surveillance includes C/A/P CT and tumor markers per schedule [LCOC-B]. CBC and chemistry profile as indicated during follow-up [OV-4, OV-6]. CA-125 or other tumor markers if elevated at diagnosis [OV-1, LCOC-12]. Genetic testing results to inform maintenance therapy decisions [OV-C]. Long-term wellness care including cancer screening for secondary malignancies [OV-6, NCCN Guidelines for Survivorship]. Symptom management for chronic toxicities (e.g., neuropathy, fatigue) [OV-D]. Psychosocial support and distress screening [OV-4].
